topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box smelled faintly of old pencil shavings and lavender sachets. Liam stared at the contents with a slack jaw. Inside, neatly stacked, were every single assignment from his entire middle school career. Math proofs, essay drafts, poorly drawn diagrams—all preserved, labeled with his name in his mother’s elegant, looping script. A hot flush crept up his neck, turning his cheeks a painful red. He wanted to snatch the box and shove it under the bed, but his fingers felt clumsy, heavy. He just stood there, the silence in the room amplifying the frantic drumming in his chest. He could feel the weight of every forgotten effort pressing down on him.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Sarah whispered, the sound catching in her throat. She picked up a crumpled history paper from the pile. The ink was faded, the handwriting shaky. A strange, tight knot formed in her stomach, making her shoulders tense up near her ears. She felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to look anywhere but at the papers, focusing instead on a dust mote dancing in a sunbeam. When she finally forced her eyes back to the stack, the sheer volume of unacknowledged work made her want to sink into the floorboards. She nervously smoothed a crease on a worksheet, her breathing shallow and quick.

[story 3]

Mark walked into the study, intending only to retrieve a misplaced book, but the sight stopped him dead in his tracks. On the mahogany desk, tucked between a stack of utility bills, was a binder. It was filled, meticulously organized, with every piece of schoolwork he had ever submitted. A cold wave washed over him, chilling him to the bone. He gripped the edge of the desk until his knuckles went white. He swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making it difficult to speak. He felt a profound sense of exposure, as if every moment of his academic life had been laid bare for inspection.
